sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Larry, has been experiencing chronic back pain for approximately 2 years, which he attributes to moving furniture one weekend.
The pain is described as sharp and stabbing, radiating down to his right leg and foot, specifically to his big toe.
He also reports numbness and tingling in his right leg, particularly to his foot, and difficulty finding a comfortable position, especially when sitting.
He denies any loss of sensation in the genital or rectal area and weakness, but experiences some numbness and tingling.
He has tried physical therapy and strong pain medications in the past, but with temporary relief.
He has gained weight due to decreased activity level.
Past medical history: The patient has no known family members with spine conditions and quit smoking 20 years ago.
He used to be active and enjoy playing golf, but his back pain has set him back.
Review of systems: The patient reports difficulty with activities due to back pain, but no other symptoms.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient has no overlying erythema or ecchymosis on his back, but exhibits midline tenderness at L4-L5 disk space and right-sided lumbar paravertebral tenderness.
The patient experiences pain with lumbar flexion and a positive straight leg test.
The neurological examination reveals decreased sensation to light touch at the right lateral thigh at L4 dermatome, with symmetrical patellar and Achilles reflexes.
Test Results: MRI results show disk herniation with nerve impingement.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient's symptoms are consistent with an L4-L5 herniated disk with radiculopathy.
Problem list: Herniated disk L4-L5 with radiculopathy; Chronic lumbar back pain.
Plan: I recommended a spinal injection with a corticosteroid to help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain.
The injection will be performed under fluoroscopy to accurately target the area of pain.
I will monitor the patient's response to the injection and schedule follow-up appointments as needed, with a maximum of four injections per year.
Follow-up: The patient will return for a follow-up appointment after the injection to assess its effectiveness.
